methods.description: To identify significant differences in Yearly Biomass Increment (YBI) between years, a pairwise comparison of YBI for single trees with a two-way ANOVA (tree × year) and Bonferroni correction was applied.

steps.description: A pairwise comparison of YBI for single trees with a two-way ANOVA (tree × year) and Bonferroni correction was applied in SPSS.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-way ANOVA with Bonferroni correction on YBI data

methods.description: To assess and remove the trend of decreasing YBI with time, which could be due to factors like fertilization treatment or age-related decline.

steps.description: Remove the trend of decreasing YBI with time before comparisons to NEP and GPP.
steps.searchable_summary: remove temporal trend from YBI data

methods.description: To assess the period of accumulated NEP or GPP on which the YBI depended, linear regressions were performed between YBI and GPP or NEP.

steps.description: Perform linear regressions between YBI and GPP or NEP calculated for all possible periods starting from April of the year before the YBI value to October of the year of the YBI value with a 1-month time resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression between YBI and GPP/NEP over specified periods

steps.description: Residuals were checked for normality using a Shapiro-Wilk test.
steps.searchable_summary: check regression residuals for normality using Shapiro-Wilk test

methods.description: Sampling and measurement of tree growth parameters including diameter, height, and tree-ring widths for biomass calculation.

steps.description: In December 2002, 15 pines and 15 spruces were sampled for growth determination.
Measure diameter of candidate trees along transects west and southwest from the tower.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tree diameter along specified transects

steps.description: Take two tree cores, 90° apart, at breast height with an increment borer.
steps.searchable_summary: extract tree cores at breast height

steps.description: Measure tree-ring widths on an Aniol-measuring machine with a binocular microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tree-ring widths using Aniol-measuring machine

steps.description: Crossdate tree rings using t value and Gleichläufigkeit statistical tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform crossdating of tree rings using statistical tests

steps.description: Assign tree heights for each year using standard height development curves.
steps.searchable_summary: model tree height development using standard curves

steps.description: Calculate biomass components from height and diameter using allometric functions.

methods.description: Measurement and analysis of net ecosystem exchange (NEE) and related parameters using a sonic anemometer and infrared gas analyser.

steps.description: Measure NEE at a height of 35 m using a SOLENT three-dimensional sonic anemometer and a LI-6262 infrared gas analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: measure NEE using sonic anemometer and gas analyser

steps.description: Calculate fluxes based on 30-min averages and correct using standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and correct fluxes from 30-min averages

steps.description: Fit regression functions to 2-month periods of nighttime NEE values.
steps.searchable_summary: fit regression functions to nighttime NEE data

steps.description: Extrapolate functions to daytime period and calculate GPP as daytime R minus daytime NEE.
Replace gaps in NEE up to 2 h with linear interpolation and model longer gaps with a light-response function.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate short gaps and model long gaps in NEE data

steps.description: Calculate Net Ecosystem Production (NEP) as -1 × NEE and sum NEP and GPP for 1-month periods.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate NEP and sum NEP and GPP monthly
